notes:
IFC-271 Account Domain Model Fixes — remediates four findings from docs/audit/account-detail-wiring-audit.md sections 11-13 (D-01, B-15, B-16, B-06) on a worktree branched from origin/main@d8d154230.

D-01 (AccountDeletedEvent): new domain event 'account.deleted' carrying accountId/name/ownerId/deletedBy, mirroring AccountCreatedEvent + the actor parity of the other account mutation events. Raised by a new aggregate command Account.markAsDeleted(deletedBy) (void, like removeParent/categorizeIndustry — deletion has no aggregate-internal invariant; cross-aggregate rules stay in the service). AccountService.deleteAccount() emits + publishes ONLY after accountRepository.delete() succeeds (publish-after-commit per packages/domain/CLAUDE.md); a delete blocked by the contacts/active-opportunities rules emits nothing. deletedBy defaults to a 'system' actor so unattributed deletions are never undefined; the router threads typedCtx.tenant.userId.

B-15 (toJSON tenantId): additive field; the two existing serialization tests assert fields individually (not exact-shape), and no API response path consumes toJSON (mapAccountToResponse reads the getter directly).

B-16 (updateAccountInfo Result): the discarded domain Result is now captured and propagated (Result.fail) before persistence, so an invalid website surfaces as a failed Result instead of a silent success.

B-06 (non-null assertion): account.router setParent replaced typedCtx.user!.userId with the guaranteed-non-null typedCtx.tenant.userId (synced from the authenticated user by tenantProcedure, already the convention at every other call site in the file) — a type-safety/consistency fix, behaviour-preserving post-middleware.

Validation: 4/4 (TypeScript, Tests, Lint, Build) green on the affected workspaces. Tests: @intelliflow/domain 2717, @intelliflow/application 1392, @intelliflow/api 6130 passed (2 skipped, pre-existing). Touched-file coverage >=90% (Account 95.9% / AccountEvents 97.8% / AccountService 93.0%); diff coverage effectively 100% (every added line exercised). Hexagonal boundary preserved (dependency-cruiser clean). An independent backend-architect review pass confirmed all four fixes and flagged the stale 1-arg deleteAccount test calls, resolved by the 'system' default. Out of scope and deliberately untouched: events-worker handler/DOMAIN_EVENT_TYPES registration (IFC-272), router update field-drop/schema-key (IFC-270), list-page type safety (IFC-273).

REBASE + GATE REMEDIATION (2026-06-11): rebased feat/ifc-271 onto current main (origin/main@8c32ece6f), which added a local codex semantic-review pre-push gate, a sonarjs/slow-regex ESLint rule, and stricter diff-coverage. No merge conflicts (my account files were disjoint from main's tools/eslint/husky changes). The codex semantic-review gate raised two MEDIUM findings: (1) Account.updateAccountInfo mutated the name before validating the website (non-atomic command) — a REAL bug, fixed by validating the website first then applying mutations, with a new domain atomicity test and a strengthened service B-16 test (name + invalid website); domain tests 2717 -> 2718. (2) eventType 'account.deleted' vs the audit-handler 'AccountDeleted' key — a FALSE POSITIVE (dot.case is the universal domain-event convention; the PascalCase key is wired by IFC-272), waived in tools/audit/codex-review-waivers.yaml. codex-review gate now PASS (0 unwaived). Lint --force clean (no slow-regex hits in the account diff). Full pre-ship re-run including codex-review.
